item.content:encoded:
This really isn't "my" kind of SCP, but for what it is it's intriguing and tells a nice story. +1 because it's a solid first entry with obvious attention to detail and research.

Two main gripes:

1. Even if the entity is as harmless as this one, the Foundation still wouldn't want to risk anyone stumbling across it. If they know for sure that the anomaly only occurs on the 30th, then I imagine they would increase security or do something special with the location on the 30th. Might be something to consider adding to the containment procedures.
2. I wonder if the Old English needs to be translated. The basic meaning is clear enough as it is, and not translating it gives us a bit of mystery to work with.

And one curious question:

1. If the anomaly only occurs on the 30th, but also repeats itself once activated (as we see at the end of the log), then what happens when the clock strikes midnight? Does the anomaly just cease to exist at whatever point is was at? Or does something else happen?

Thanks for the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for being a simple yet interesting read.

Also, I think there's a typographical error at the footnotes. It says disapora but I think it should be diaspora? Correct me if I'm wrong.

Good job, nonetheless! :)

item.title: Re: +1, But--
item.content:encoded: Thank you kindly; thanks for the suggestions. Added!

item.title: +1, But--
item.content:encoded:
First and foremost, +1. I like the simple, but anomalous nature that tells a story.

However, some small nitpicks I have:

1) At "…behaviors and movements1which lasts approximately…", the footnote connects both words, rather than having a space in-between.
2) At "Canadian society viewed biracial marriage overwhelmingly negative…", this sentence breaks it flow a little. I'd change "negative" to "negatively" to keep the flow going.
3) Does SCP-4418 only manifest on October 30, with nothing else happening at any other time? If so, I would mention such in the description, briefly — "SCP-4418 does not exhibit any anomalous behaviors on any other date, per testing done on [dates]/[redactions]/etc."

Still, I think it's a good article and a solid addition overall to the wiki. I really like the concept.
I'm not always into really simple stories like this, but this was actually quite well done! Easy to follow and understand, and it's a good story in of itself. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it, it means a lot. This originally ended at them dancing, but I scarpped it due to it not really leading anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pathos was highly visible but it did a decent job in spite of it. I liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a SCP-XXXX on the top of the test log

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here we go! Hopefully second time is the charm! Special thanks to OriCat, Anastasia Vixley and TheMightyMcB for all the help. Very Greatful!

Image of the homestead is from https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rowley,_Alberta

The couple is from here: https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Louis-and-Louisa-Gregory.png

First image is CC complient, and the second is in the public domain.
